mentions.note: Held through weakness despite SaaS headwinds; Einstein AI and $50B buyback signal conviction; asymmetrical risk/reward favors holding over selling.
Best performing Dow stock (up 66% YTD); Silicon One and Acacia acquisitions provide exclusive hyperscaler connectivity; quantum-safe infrastructure roadmap.
CEO Jeff Simmons and company 'come on very strong'; preferred over Zoetis in animal health sector.
Rated as 'terrific situation'; inexpensive valuation with good dividend yield.
10 commercialized billion-dollar drugs, no major patent expirations, growing medtech via acquisitions (Shockwave, Abiomed). P/E 19x. Buy slowly; undervalued amid sector rotation.
Fortress balance sheet, 13x earnings (below market average), best-in-class franchise. Contrarian value entry amid credit fears; buy and hold, not a momentum play.
Faith-based play: CEO Steve Cahill (Kellogg turnaround artist) tackling legacy cost structure. 6.85% yield, high-risk; portfolio hedge if tech momentum breaks and dividend holds.
Premier staple (Kleenex, paper towels, diapers), 13x earnings, 5.25% safe yield. Merging with Kenvue; CEO Michael Sue executing synergies. Defensive anchor as growth stocks stumble.
New GLP-1 formulation may be first to shed fat without muscle loss; could deliver knockout punch vs. Novo Nordisk if approved. Bull case compelling despite shareholder overlap concern.
Missed a quarter (rare event) but recovery likely underway. Cast aside amid discretionary weakness; contrarian value play alongside QSR peers in beaten-down sector.
Caller Frank noted stock at 83 (down 11%) seems low given streaming dominance; Cramer committed to deep-dive analysis but stopped short of buy recommendation pending investigation.
CEO declared 'SaaS apocalypse' dead; AI drives consolidation/platformization demand. CyberArk acquisition in pole position; major wins ($200M, $80M deals). Duopoly with CrowdStrike.
$600M free cash flow, 30% EPS growth; focused strategy on top brands (Kay, Jared, Blue Nile); digital experience upgrade and centralized cost structure.
Pizza Hut sale talks (African capital) will unlock value; KFC and Taco Bell are 'amazing performers.' Free of Pizza Hut drag, stock should re-rate much higher.
Cramer cites unspecified concerns ('something not well there'); prefers competitor Elanco with stronger momentum.
